home.title: Image Shuttle
home.subtitle: The free, private image studio in your browser — compress, convert, edit, resize, collage and design. No uploads, no watermark.
home.feature1Desc: All processing happens in your browser. No files are ever uploaded to any server.
home.feature2Desc: Reduce file size by up to 80% while maintaining visual quality. Supports JPEG, PNG, WebP, and AVIF.
home.feature3Desc: Convert between JPEG, PNG, WebP, and AVIF formats with a single click.
home.feature4Desc: Compress or convert multiple images at once with intelligent queue management.
home.feature5Desc: Choose from High Quality, Balanced, or Maximum Compression presets — or fine-tune manually.
home.feature6Desc: Compare original and compressed images side-by-side with a slider preview.
home.step1Desc: Drag and drop or click to select your images
home.step2Desc: Choose quality, format, and size settings
home.step3Desc: Preview the result and download compressed files
home.privacyBadge: Your files never leave your device
home.whyCompressionP1: Image compression is one of the most impactful optimizations you can make for website performance. Large, uncompressed images are the number one cause of slow page loads, which directly affects user experience, bounce rates, and search engine rankings. Google's Core Web Vitals — particularly Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) — measure how quickly the main content of a page loads, and images are often the largest content elements. By compressing your images before uploading them to your website, you can reduce LCP by 50% or more, which translates to better SEO rankings and higher user engagement.
home.whyCompressionP2: Beyond web performance, image compression saves bandwidth and storage space. If you manage a blog, e-commerce site, or social media presence, you likely handle hundreds of images every month. Compressing these images reduces your hosting costs, speeds up backups, and makes your site more accessible to users on slower mobile connections. Image Shuttle makes this process effortless by running entirely in your browser — no need to install software or upload files to third-party servers.
home.howDifferentTitle: How Image Shuttle Differs from Server-Based Tools
home.howDifferentP1: Traditional image compression tools like TinyPNG and Compressor.io require you to upload your images to their servers for processing. While convenient, this approach raises privacy concerns — your photos, designs, and documents pass through third-party infrastructure. For businesses handling sensitive content, this can be a compliance issue. Image Shuttle takes a fundamentally different approach: all processing happens locally in your browser using the Canvas API, WebAssembly, and Web Workers. Your files never leave your device, making it ideal for privacy-conscious users and organizations.
home.howDifferentP2: Image Shuttle also stands out with its batch processing capabilities. While many free tools limit you to one image at a time, Image Shuttle uses your device's CPU cores (via navigator.hardwareConcurrency) to process multiple images in parallel. Combined with support for JPEG, PNG, WebP, and AVIF formats, plus a real-time before/after comparison slider powered by react-compare-slider, Image Shuttle provides a professional-grade compression workflow without any cost or registration.
home.compressSubtitle: Batch compress and convert JPEG, PNG, WebP and AVIF — drop files below to start.
home.compressEditorHint: Want to crop, resize to exact pixels, or add text to one photo?
home.modulesSubtitle: Compressor, photo editor, collage maker and design studio — all on-device, all free, no watermark.

about.heroSubtitle: A free, private, browser-based image studio by ShuttleLab — compress, edit, collage and design
about.serviceP1: Image Shuttle is a free online image studio that runs entirely in your browser. It started as a compressor and has grown into four tools in one: a compressor/converter for JPEG, PNG, WebP and AVIF; a photo editor with adjustments, filters, crop and exact-pixel resize; a collage maker with 20 grid layouts; and a design studio with layers, templates and free custom canvas sizes. Unlike traditional online tools, nothing is ever uploaded — all processing uses the Canvas API, WebAssembly and Web Workers on your device.
about.serviceP2: Need a 512×512 avatar? Crop to 1:1, type 512 into Resize, and export — at exactly that size, with the format and compression quality you choose. There is never a watermark, never an account requirement, and custom canvas sizes that other tools paywall are free here. Whether you are optimizing images for the web, annotating screenshots, building social covers or stitching photos into a grid, Image Shuttle handles it with zero privacy risk.
about.safeDesc: All image processing happens locally in your browser. No files are uploaded, no data is collected, and no third parties can access your images.
about.privacyDesc: Start compressing immediately — no sign-up, no email, no tracking. Just drop your images and go.
about.ttlDesc: Image Shuttle is open source under the MIT license, built on modern web standards with all processing done client-side.
about.fastDesc: Leveraging Web Workers for parallel processing and WebAssembly for PNG optimization, Image Shuttle delivers professional-grade compression at native speed.
about.use1: Web developers optimizing images for faster page loads
about.use2: Designers converting between formats for different platforms
about.use3: Content creators batch-compressing images for social media
about.use4: Anyone who needs to reduce image file size without losing quality
about.contactDesc: Have questions, feedback, or need support?
about.supportTitle: Meeting you for the first time feels like the return of an old friend
about.supportDesc: Image Shuttle is crafted with care by ShuttleLab — completely free and open source. If it has helped you compress even a single image, sharing it with friends who need it is the best support we could ask for.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.intro: At Image Shuttle, your privacy is our core value. This Privacy Policy explains our approach to data collection and processing when you use image.shuttlelab.org.
privacyPage.section1Content: Image Shuttle does not collect, store, or transmit any of your images or personal data. All image processing occurs entirely within your web browser using client-side technologies (Canvas API, WebAssembly, and Web Workers). Your images never leave your device.
privacyPage.section2Content: Image Shuttle uses your browser's localStorage to store your preferences, such as theme selection (light/dark/system) and language settings (English/Chinese). This data remains on your device and is never transmitted to our servers.
privacyPage.section3Content: Unlike traditional online image compression tools, Image Shuttle performs all operations locally in your browser. There is no server-side processing, no file uploads, and no temporary storage on remote servers. This design ensures complete privacy for your images.
privacyPage.section4Content: Image Shuttle is hosted on Cloudflare for content delivery. Cloudflare may collect standard web server logs (IP address, browser type, access time) as part of normal CDN operation. We do not use analytics trackers, advertising networks, or any other third-party services that collect user data.
privacyPage.section5Content: Image Shuttle does not use cookies. All user preferences are stored in localStorage, which is not transmitted to our servers.
privacyPage.section6Content: Image Shuttle does not knowingly collect any personal information from children under 13. Since we do not collect any personal data at all, our service is safe for users of all ages.
privacyPage.section7Content: We may update this Privacy Policy periodically. Any changes will be reflected in the 'Last updated' date above. Your continued use of Image Shuttle after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.
privacyPage.section8Content: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at support@shuttlelab.org.

termsPage.title: Terms of Service
termsPage.intro: Welcome to Image Shuttle. These Terms of Service govern your use of image.shuttlelab.org. By accessing or using Image Shuttle, you agree to be bound by these terms.
termsPage.section1Content: Image Shuttle is a free, browser-based image compression and format conversion tool. All processing occurs locally in your browser — no files are uploaded to our servers. The service is provided as-is by ShuttleLab at no cost to the user.
termsPage.section2Content: By using Image Shuttle, you represent that you are at least 13 years old and have the authority to enter into this agreement. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, you must not use our service.
termsPage.section3Content: We grant you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to access and use Image Shuttle for personal and professional purposes. You may use the tool to compress and convert images you own or have permission to process.
termsPage.section4Content: You are responsible for the images you process through Image Shuttle. You agree to only process images you own or have legal permission to modify. You must not use the service for any illegal purpose.
termsPage.section5Content: Image Shuttle and its original content, features, and functionality are owned by ShuttleLab. The service is an independent implementation built on open web standards and open-source libraries.
termsPage.section6Content: Image Shuttle is provided 'as is' without warranties of any kind. We do not guarantee that the service will be uninterrupted, error-free, or produce specific compression results. Image quality and file size reduction depend on the input image and chosen settings.
termsPage.section7Content: ShuttleLab shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising from your use of Image Shuttle, including but not limited to loss of data, loss of profits, or business interruption.
termsPage.section8Content: Image Shuttle may contain links to other ShuttleLab products or third-party websites. We are not responsible for the content or practices of these external sites.
termsPage.section9Content: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Changes will be effective immediately upon posting. Your continued use of Image Shuttle after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
termsPage.section10Content: For questions about these Terms, contact us at support@shuttlelab.org.
